name: BRAINSTORM CELL THERAPEUTICS INC.
business: Brainstorm Cell Therapeutics Inc. develops autologous cellular therapies for neurodegenerative diseases, primarily NurOwn for amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS), progressive multiple sclerosis (PMS), and Alzheimer's disease (AD). The company has not generated any revenues from operations and is dependent on regulatory approval and commercialization of its product candidates.
sellsTo: healthcare providers specializing in neurodegenerative diseases
namedSuppliers: City of Hope's Center for Biomedicine and Genetics
Connell and O'Reilly Cell Manipulation Core Facility at Dana Farber Cancer Institute

exposures.sentence: Brainstorm Cell Therapeutics Inc. is a leading biotechnology company committed to the development and commercialization of best-in-class autologous cellular therapies for the treatment of neurodegenerative diseases, including amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS), progressive multiple sclerosis (PMS), Alzheimer s disease (AD), and other neurodegenerative diseases.

The exosome research efforts are primarily focused on manufacturing of MSC-NTF exosomes from bone marrow derived MSC: 1. Developing and optimizing large scale cell culture processes using bioreactors, to generate exosomes. 2. Developing advanced scalable purification GMP methods that can be applied to commercial use.

catalysts.event: FDA granted written agreement under a Special Protocol Assessment (SPA) for Phase 3b trial of NurOwn in ALS
catalysts.sentence: On April 9, 2024, the Company announced that it received written agreement from the FDA, under a SPA, on the design for a Phase 3b trial of NurOwn in ALS.

catalysts.event: FDA alignment on Chemistry, Manufacturing, and Controls (CMC) for Phase 3b clinical trial of NurOwn
catalysts.sentence: On June 26, 2024, the Company announced that it has reached alignment with FDA on the Chemistry, Manufacturing, and Controls (CMC) aspects of Brainstorm s Phase 3b clinical trial for NurOwn, its investigational therapy for ALS.

catalysts.event: FDA advisory committee meeting voted that NurOwn did not demonstrate substantial evidence of effectiveness for mild to moderate ALS
catalysts.sentence: On September 27, 2023 we announced that the Advisory Committee voted, with 17 voting no, one voting yes, and one abstention, that NurOwn did not demonstrate substantial evidence of effectiveness for treatment of mild to moderate ALS.

catalysts.event: BLA for NurOwn was withdrawn from FDA
catalysts.sentence: The BLA was withdrawn on November 3, 2023.

catalysts.event: FDA consideration of a Citizen Petition requesting a new review of data supporting NurOwn
catalysts.sentence: On July 8, 2025, the Company acknowledged that the FDA consideration of a Citizen Petition requesting a new review of the data supporting NurOwn will provide a critical opportunity to reaffirm its potential as therapy for ALS.
